This is a review from a 20 years ago, take it for what it is worth. I hired them to defend me on a felony marijuana charge. The charge was HS 11359, intent to sell marijuana. The initial Public Defender had told me that I would have to plea guilty to a felony and would get no additional jail time, only what I had already served and community service followed by two years formal probation. I did not like the prospect of becoming a convicted felon so I sought out a new attorney, which ultimately was Dennis Alan Lempert. Long story short, but after going back to court with a DAL attorney representing me I was offered the exact same deal which I was urged to take. I was FINALLY told that the charge was a non alternative felony in Santa Clara County which meant I was not eligible for deferred entry of judgment which would reduce the felony to a misdemeanor after 18 months if I stayed out of trouble. It is partly my fault for not looking into it more initially but that is why I hired an attorney, they are suppose to know the laws, which I believe they did. They were shown the entire police report before I hired them and they told me they could definitely help me. Unless they were entirely incompetent, which I don't believe, they knew full well they could not get the charge reduced. They said they could help, and they did, they helped themselves to my hard earned money, that is it.

Dennis Lempert is very prompt,eager and motivated to bring the best defense representation I was very please with him he is well known thru out Santa Clara co. Court systems also former DA for Santa Clara co. A lil pricey but you pay for what you get!!!

reviews.ownerResponse:
This review is misrepresentative and follows our resigning and concerned dealings with this paralegal. We were retained for only four hours following a free initial consult to try to negotiate her Labor Commission loss against a major corporation and to attend her trial-setting conference.

The reviewer completely omits that when she became initially hostile, we offered to resign, issue her a refund, and were threatened if we did not remain on. These are serious omissions in a review claiming misrepresentation for a few hours’ fees.

We timely reached out to her former employer, which had not yet responded to our initial correspondence, as her appeals case was very new and the attorney who successfully represented the company had not replied to us as of the trial setting conference. When asking this client whom she tried the case against, she did not know, but became highly critical of my staff. We identified that attorney through prior work with that company, which has hundreds of attorneys, and had settlement discussions on her two claims, during which they related their own challenged dealings with her and proposed possibly settling both cases together, subject to that attorney’s obtaining authority. This proposal was still pending when I was forced to quit due to continued hostile interactions with the client.

My dealings with the reviewer were very limited as she dealt with 2 of my staff and I kept my interactions with her in writing. She now characterizes my interactions with her positions as “rude”, however, these primarily remained in writing given my concerns. We were not her first attorneys in the matter and her past attorneys also reportedly resigned.

Following the trial setting conference 6 days ago, we provided our written statement for our time which was now exhausted, and I offered to continue, asking for no fees while awaiting the company’s reply. We then received further hostile correspondence from the client and, with this breakdown in the professional relationship, I resigned. Having earlier declined a refund and insisting I continue to complete the court conference, the challenging emails which I received were so compromising that I had no choice but to resign.

We clearly regret this reviewer’s continued misperceptions; however, we do wish her well ahead. Our successful handling of thousands of cases over decades of service in the public good best reflects our clients’ experiences, as well as the fact that they continue to refer their family members, coworkers, congregants, and neighbors for help. This alone is the best measure of our successful practice, for which I am grateful.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We are disappointed to read Mr. McClure’s review, which omits our substantial efforts. We were specifically hired to try to settle his case but not litigate it due to the defense allegations raised against Mr. McClure. Without addressing these challenges, Mr. McClure was initially interviewed by us, his claims evaluated, correspondence was prepared to his employer, and a Complaint was filed before the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, a prerequisite in discrimination suits. There was some initial delay with the EEOC due to the Covid backlog, and we next prepared Mr. McClure and participated in his EEOC interview. The company did reply to the EEOC, with the response being sent only to Mr. McClure by the EEOC. Importantly, he did not initially inform us of this. During our follow-up, we learned of these events, evaluated the defenses, and prepared a further rebuttal to the EEOC and directly to the employer, evaluating the company reply and presenting a settlement demand after conferring with the client.

Ultimately, the company declined to negotiate, the EEOC did not find sufficient evidence to sustain the claims and dismissed the charges. All efforts to generate settlement interest with the company failed. Their defenses and risk moving forward were clearly understood as a risk by Mr. McClure. Contrary to the review, we exceeded all attorney time billed, as we continued to attempt to generate settlement even after the company declined an offer.

It is true that no attorney can guarantee a result and, here, there was a substantial factual dispute amid other concerns raised against Mr. McClure. We sincerely tried to assist this individual, but settlement takes two, and the attorney’s inability to guarantee a result on the facts presented was raised in writing at the time we were retained. We have an excellent record of successful prosecutions and mediated settlements, as best reflected in our over 40 top reviews on Avvo.com and our perfect “10” rating. We share Mr. McClure’s disappointment and wish this reviewer the best ahead.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Mary, we are so disappointed to learn of how you perceived us, and alarmed that you would write a review where you were not a client, received no services, had not met with our attorneys, and drew your conclusions apparently based solely on one early morning very brief return call where you were informed that we did not feel that the engagement was "going to work", with your interpreting this to have been "rude".

reviews.text: Nicholas saranto is probably the best lawer I've spoken to all my life. I really mean this, he's helped me with non-disclosure and expungement of my record. In a timely manner as well. I was contacted by this attorney after being referred through a State Bar referral service. Initially, the voicemail and introduction were professional and gave me a positive first impression.

Unfortunately, the call quickly went downhill. Very early on, the attorney claimed to already understand my case without asking several key questions that would reasonably be expected in a family law consultation. When I attempted to clarify or provide context, the conversation turned into what felt more like a lecture than a consultation. Comments such as “whoa,” “pump the brakes,” and repeated interruptions made it difficult to get a word in.

The tone became increasingly dismissive, with statements like “no, no, no — this is this type of case,” despite limited fact-gathering. I ultimately let him speak uninterrupted, at which point the conversation abruptly shifted to billing. I was told the case would require a $12,000 fee, framed as reasonable because “it’s not $10k and other firms charge $15k.” There was no clear explanation of scope, strategy, or why that number made sense for my situation — just a comparison to higher numbers elsewhere.

I then raised reasonable concerns about publicly available information: mixed reviews, limited time practicing in California, and lack of specialization in the specific area of law I was seeking help with. I asked how he would address those concerns and explain why hiring him would make sense. The response was dismissive, characterizing negative reviews as coming from “a few angry or upset people,” followed by the comment that this was not “shit on Nick day.”

At that point, I ended the conversation and thanked him for his time.

Overall, the experience felt unprofessional and overly familiar in tone, rather than client-centered or consultative. If this is how communication is handled with potential clients, I would be concerned about professionalism in higher-stakes settings. Zero stars if that were an option.

I would also caution others that a State Bar referral does not appear to vet for professionalism or communication style. Only licensure, I suppose. Maybe Nick was having a rough start to 2026.

Case number RG19002294, Alameda County Superior Court, California.
JAMS Fee arbitration. Ref No. 5460000673
I had an employment case. My first lawyer left my case and she put a lien on my case in order to get her fees. I hired Grellas Shah LLP to finish up the employment case and fight the lien my first lawyer put on my case. Dhaivat Shah, David Siegel and Joseph Guzzetta participated in my case.
The KEY in my case was a term sheet, a short version of a settlement agreement, that was signed at a mediation. It was NOT signed by all parties. So the judge in employment case ruled it not enforceable. I signed it because I was “pressured” and I was lied to by my first lawyer. I was made to believe that it was just a document to sign to show we made progress because the mediation was about to end soon. The full and complete settlement agreement would be sent to me a few weeks later and if I don’t like that one, I can simply walk away from it and there would be NO settlement. But I don’t have evidence to prove that. No video with audio or written record of what I was told at the mediation.
Dhaivat told me I had a strong case (against my first lawyer who put a lien on my case for her fees). About a potential fee arbitration, he said “If you win (and I think you will win), you can seek reimbursement of your attorney's fees and other costs but it is not guaranteed that you will get 100% of them reimbursed.” He gave my case to Joseph Guzzetta, who represented me in the fee arbitration.
About the cost of the fee arbitration to remove my first lawyer’s lien on my case, Dhaivat said “It could be 20-30k." unless the other party tries to expand the issues and the judge doesn't stop her. He later said it could be 20-30k if it is a narrow arbitration. But when I questioned him about the cost later he told me my arbitration was not a “narrow” one. The real cost, the fees Grellas Shah billed me, exceeded 120K.
I was given a good offer before the fee arbitration, Dhaivat did recommend to take that offer. I made decisions based on the wrong information I got. E.g. I had a strong case and the cost would be low unless the other party escalates. I gave a counter offer. But the other party went straight to fee arbitration and I lost all the money in dispute and I had to pay attorney’s fee for both sides. A loss of about 440K. I could have got about 210K had I accepted their last offer before the arbitration. In the end, I filed for bankruptcy.
We didn’t want the signed termsheet to be admitted as evidence, but the judge ruled against me. It was NOT signed by all parties to settle the employment case, but it was still a binding contract, I learned this myself during the hearing. Too late! Imagine the SHOCK I got!
I made my decisions based on wrong info i was given, e.g. the strength of my case (I got a bad case ACTUALLY), the REAL cost etc.

I checked the bills and I believed some hours billed to be questionable so I reached out to Grellas Shah LLP and said I had plans about fee arbitration and maybe more. The law firm terminated our attorney client relation immediately!
(I wrote this review on 4/25) I celebrate my birthday on 4/25 and today I’m turning 40 and my “birthday gift” is the "order of discharge" letter of my bankruptcy case. I am doing my PhD, so I get only a little money a month as stipend. I had a temporary working permit and worked for a company about 3 years before I was wrongfully terminated. I constantly worked overtime but I was never compensated for those overtime hours. So I worked hard and got paid little for about 3 years, then the employment case and this fee arbitration case lasted about 4 years and maybe a little more. In total, more than 7 years, and the little money I saved , about 30k or so, by eating cheap food, like McChicken (it was 1 dollar apiece back then), ramen noodles etc. and the pain and frustration and anger etc all these sufferings. In the end, my life got ruined.

For context, I rarely post reviews and up until this review, all of them were 5 star reviews because it can be hard to run a business and I have real sympathy for business owners.  However, a recent conversation with some friends inspired me to post my first and likely only negative review on Google – I’m speaking up because picking a meal is one thing, picking a lawyer you’re spending thousands of dollars on is another.  People can have a hard time finding a great lawyer at an affordable price, especially as it’s hard for them to evaluate the quality of legal representation, and I hope my perspective can help out those that are looking.

First, some background on my interactions with Mr. Pascoe: my partner and I were looking for lawyers to draft a prenup – I hired one lawyer based on a reddit rec while my partner hired Mr. Pascoe.  However, we both agreed that if given the choice again, my partner would never have hired Mr. Pascoe.

I’ll refrain from going into detail on everything I saw that concerned me, but suffice it to say that I would not recommend Mr. Pascoe to anyone based on what I saw of the prenup drafting, his interpretations of the law, how long it took to get things done, and more.  Here’s just one example of a major problem in the prenup drafting - Mr. Pascoe (or someone working at his direction) had removed and then inserted clauses into the prenup that actively hurt their own client’s financial interests.  In a few different places, their revised prenup stated that after marriage our assets would be community property rather than separate property – what this meant was that my partner, who makes more money than I do, would have had his post-marriage income, stocks that vested to him, his retirement money, and more split 50/50 with me in the event we both divorced rather than considered his own separate property which I was not entitled to.  This was not what either of us wanted (plus if we wanted that, it’s the default in CA so there would be no need for a prenup), and I was genuinely astonished that Mr. Pascoe’s revision changed the correct delineation of these assets as separate property in the original prenup draft my lawyer sent to Mr. Pascoe.  If I were to be charitable, perhaps they would have caught this massive error in the second (and last) revision they made – however, I’m doubtful they would have noticed an error of 3 words in a 20 plus page document given other errors I saw in their revisions (including their last one).

Again, I emphasize that my review is specific to Mr. Pascoe.  Every lawyer is different and this review is not meant to be about lawyers whose legal work I’ve obviously not had the chance to see.

Hopefully my experience and perspective will be helpful to anyone who reads this review.

Mercy came highly recommended from an attorney friend. She is passionate about what she does and really cares about you as a client.  The first day I met Mercy, we spoke for an hour just getting to know each other. She is charismatic, funny, and quite the storyteller. I immediately felt at ease having chosen the perfect person to represent us in a rather daunting, bureaucratic process.  My partner and I are currently navigating the K-1 visa process and thanks to Mercy have just received our approval!

Our case presented some rather unique challenges.  USCIS notoriously asks follow up questions on every case so be diligent in checking your case number online on a monthly basis to see if there is any activity.  USCIS stated that they sent a letter to me in January 2024 and neither myself nor Mercy received the request for more information. I happened to check the case in April 2024 (12 months after we had filed the petition) to find that our case was on the verge of being cancelled.

This is where Mercy stepped in and went above and beyond for us.  She worked tirelessly making phone calls and writing letters on our behalf pleading not to abandon our case. She even wrote to the director of USCIS! And her efforts did not go unnoticed. USCIS listened and changed our status from “Cancelled” to “Reconsidering” to “Approved.”  It was an emotional rollercoaster, but we couldn’t be more thrilled at the outcome.

I admit I considered filing the paperwork myself without representation, however, I am so grateful to have found Mercy. Her fees are reasonable, and I would not hesitate to hire her again. She is knowledgeable about immigration and has her client’s best interest at heart. If there is any issue, she will work hard to resolve it for you. You are in good hands. Thank you, Mercy!

Dear Azad,
Thank you so much for taking the time to write a review. I take all feedback seriously and wanted to respond. Yes, I have a website and listed there are the types of cases that I take on. While you and I never actually spoke, my paralegal explained to you several times that we would not be able to help you with your specific case. She let you know that I had reviewed it, and that I was not able to assist you. We offered a referral to another qualified and experienced attorney, who could help - but you insisted on speaking with me for just a few minutes, even though we said that we couldn't help. I totally understand how frustrating it is to try to hire an attorney that you've chosen. At times, attorneys are not able to explain the specific reason for why they cannot take you on as a client. You were not offered a consultation and I did not speak with you, specifically because of the rules that govern an attorney's professional and ethical obligations. You are not and were not a client reaching out with a problem.
If a future potential client would reach out to try to schedule a possible consultation, they would be invited to share a few details about themselves and their case through an intake form, so that we could conduct a proper conflicts check. And if we could help them, we would schedule a consultation, or refer them to an attorney who may be able to solve their legal issue.
I wish you the best.
Thank you,
- Dmitry

I worked with Sowards Law Firm to create my estate plan and trust, and overall I had a very positive experience.

After completing their intake form, the team truly handled everything end to end. Mr. Sowards himself took the time to walk me through the different trust structures and helped me choose the option that best fit my current situation as well as my long-term plans. That guidance was extremely valuable and gave me a lot of confidence in the decisions I was making.

Following that, Hailey and the rest of the team were outstanding—very responsive, professional, and patient. They answered multiple questions, accommodated changes during the process, and worked closely with me to ensure the trust was structured exactly the way I wanted. Once things were underway, the entire process moved quickly and smoothly, and everything was completed within about 2–3 weeks. They even accommodated an earlier signing appointment due to my travel schedule, which I really appreciated.

The only challenging part of the experience was scheduling the initial consultation. The firm is very strict about having the intake form completed by a specific deadline before the first call can be scheduled. I missed providing some information the first time, which resulted in my appointment being canceled, and the next available slot was nearly a month out. That was frustrating, although to be fair, it was due to me not submitting the requested information on time.

That said, I do think the intake process could be clearer. It wasn’t obvious which information was critical for the first meeting versus what could be completed later. Clearer guidance there would make the onboarding experience smoother.

Overall, once past the initial scheduling step, the experience was excellent. The team is knowledgeable, efficient, and genuinely cares about getting things right. I would absolutely recommend Sowards Law Firm to anyone looking for a well-thought-out estate plan and trust, especially if you value long-term planning and professional guidance

We’re equally glad that Hailey and the rest of our team were able to support you with responsiveness, professionalism, and care throughout the process.

We also sincerely appreciate your honest feedback regarding the intake and scheduling experience. You’ve highlighted a valuable area for improvement. We want you to know that we are actively reviewing both our intake correspondence and the form itself to provide clearer guidance on what information is required prior to the initial consultation. Our goal is to make the onboarding process as smooth and intuitive as the rest of the experience, and your insight is helping us do that.

Thank you again for your recommendation and for placing your trust in us. If you ever have additional questions or need support in the future, our team is always here to help.

reviews.text: I chose Sowards Law Firm because they were on the referral list from both my Fidelity and Schwab financial consultants, and they also had good reviews by ChatCPT and other AI chatbots. I found their online data portal to be a convenient tool for providing my relevant information. After providing the intake info, a (free) 30 minute zoom meeting with a legal assistant to review the information and fill in anything missing. Next was a design meeting (about 1 hour) working with the lawyer, Benjamin Sowards, to design the specifics of my trust. After that was the fee agreement. I guess I could have bailed at that point having paid nothing yet if I was not satisfied. The fee was in two half-payments. The first was before they actually started drafting the trust, and the second was before the last meeting for trust notarization and signing. The next meeting was for review of the draft trust documents, and the final meeting was for signing the trust documents. Overall, I was very satisfied with the process and the resulting trust portfolio.

I lease a 2022 Land Rover Discovery that was plague with “low battery-Please start engine” warning.
I been told multiple times by the service department that i have to drive long distance.  The third time i took the vehicle for service they will just charge the battery and send me on my way and a week later the warning comes back and the problem worsen. The trunk will not open, the key will not function, the vehicle will stall, the screen intermittently black out on me. I started to lose faith on the vehicle and i feel it did not really give me the value and performance LR promised their consumer. Before then i  was driving a 20 year old car but when i was diagnosed with Breast Cancer and my treatments are out of town long drives we decided to lease a more reliable vehicle to get to all my Medical appointments that are out of town but we end up with a very unreliable vehicle and LR service Dept. are very dismissive and lack of concern. By the 5th occurrence of the warning my frustrations turned into hopelessness.
I know there is a Lemon Law but i  have no knowledge about it just as many others until we have car problems. I started reading if my car problems qualify but i was adamant and i start looking up a California Lemon Lawyers to help me sort it out. I chance upon Atty. Scott on avvo answering lemon law questions and upon checking he has a stellar review.
The next day i decided to call his office and i spoke to the Intake specialist Leilani. She was very informative and she asked me to send her all my work orders and she will submit it for review if my case qualify. Few days later she called me by phone that they will take my case, I breathed a sigh of relief. The same day i was sent a retainer document to sign.
This is not an overnight-quick process. It takes patience and tolerance. communications are through email so that there are paper trail to go back to. However slow i am confident that the firm   is on my side. Emails are promptly returned and answer. Leilani is very thorough and informative. When my case went to the next step Breana Hildebrant gave me the minute order of my case and has been so amazing to communicate to.
Atty. Scott took on my case. He’s very generous of his knowledge, very informative and thorough. He returns emails promptly and he gives me a call when needed. He was honest and transparent. He advice me not to expect too much but he promised me that he will do his best for me and i never doubted him for a second. Sure enough he over delivers. The settlement offer was more than i expected and thats because of his expertise and many years of experience in the business.
My case took 11 months from start to settlement. A radio silent for few months its because the firm have no control over the response of the Manufacturer and the court takes a lot of time as well but when the ball start rolling Atty Scott is on it. He is the best. He would not be highly rated for no reason.
There are honest people out there and Atty. Scott, Leilani and Breana are one of them. They take pride of what they do. Its comforting to know that there are good, honest firm who will fight for you and your hard earned money and make these Car Manufacturer accountable.
Dear Atty. Scott, Leilani and Breana, Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
As i continue my battle with Breast Cancer you have no idea how appreciative i am that i am now in a position to put myself in a better vehicle. My settlement would help me pay my new car and that is something less of me to worry. I cant thank you enough but please know that you made my struggle a little easier to bear because of your dedication to your chosen profession.
Thank you so much.
Sincerely,
Noreen A.

reviews.text: I’ve known Julie Ann for many years and I emphatically recommend her.  Her knowledge, expertise, and experience in Estate planning and Tax Law are exceptional. This, combined with her ability to explain all the details in laymen’s terms, made her the ideal attorney to address our needs.

I came to Bay Area Estate Attorneys to set up my living trust, and I couldn’t have asked for a better experience. From the start, the team was professional, empathetic, and extremely thorough. Like many people, I had been putting off estate planning for years, partly due to uncertainty, partly due to dreading the legal process. They made everything straightforward and gave me total peace of mind.

They didn’t just hand me a stack of documents, they walked me through each section, answered every question (no matter how small), and made sure I understood exactly what I was signing. What really impressed me was how they balanced the legal expertise with humanity; this wasn’t just about paperwork, it was about family, clarity, and making sure I could sleep at night knowing everything was in order.

Their process was organized, their communication was top-notch, and the final product (a beautifully bound trust binder plus digital copies) was incredibly well-done. I especially appreciated how they gave me clear next steps and even helped me prepare a message to my trustees.

Highly recommend for anyone looking for a trusted estate planning partner who actually cares.
